heading: View Programmatic Access to a Project
body:
You can view the details of all service accounts or API (Application Programming Interface) keys that have access
to your project.

To view the details of a service account in a project using the
Atlas UI:

All the service accounts with access to your project are listed.

Click the name of a service account to view its details, including:

- The obfuscated client secret for the service account

- The date the client secret was last used

- The date the client secret was created

- The IP (Internet Protocol) addresses from which the service account can access the API (Application Programming Interface)

- The [project role](project-roles) the service account has been assigned

You can use the Atlas Administration API to [list the names
and details of the service accounts that have access to your
project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-/listprojectserviceaccounts).

To list all API keys for your project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas projects apiKeys list [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas projects apiKeys list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-projects-apiKeys-list).

You can view the API (Application Programming Interface) access list entries for a project
API (Application Programming Interface) key using an `atlas organizations` command.

To list IP access list entries for your API key using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists list <apiKeyID> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-accessLists-list).

To view the details of an API (Application Programming Interface) key in a project using the
Atlas UI:

- Click  to the right of the API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

- Click View Details.

The \<Public Key> API Key Details modal displays the following information:

- The obfuscated Private Key

- The date the key was last used

- The date the key was created

- The IP (Internet Protocol) address or addresses from which the API (Application Programming Interface) key can
  access the API (Application Programming Interface)
